name: dumbbell fly on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your head, neck, and upper back are supported by the ball.
Extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing each other.
Bend your elbows slightly and lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and squeeze your chest muscles as you bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Sit on an exercise ball and hold a dumbbell in each hand. Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your head, neck, and upper back are supported by the ball. Extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing each other. Bend your elbows slightly and lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest. Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and squeeze your chest muscles as you bring the dumbbells back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
